title: Stratified K-Fold
concept: Stratified K-Fold cross-validation preserves the percentage of each class in every fold, ensuring stable evaluation for rare categories.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main benefit of K-Fold cross-validation over a single train/validation split?

options.text: It reduces evaluation variance by averaging scores across different validation folds.
It automatically makes the model train twice as fast.
It eliminates the need for any training data.

exercises.explain: Correct! K-Fold averages out the luck of a single random split. But what if some categories of our target are extremely rare?

cards.title: The empty fold
cards.body: Imagine you're building a rare disease detector. Only 1% of patients in your dataset have the disease. If you split your data randomly into 10 folds, some folds might end up with zero disease cases! How can you validate on a fold with no examples to find?

cards.title: What is Stratified K-Fold?
cards.body: Stratified K-Fold is a variation of K-Fold where each fold is made to contain approximately the same percentage of samples for each target class as the complete dataset.

cards.title: The recipe blend
cards.body: Think of your dataset as a chocolate chip cookie dough. If you split it into 5 chunks, you want to make sure every cookie gets a fair share of chocolate chips. Stratifying is like making sure each chunk has the same chip-to-dough ratio.

cards.title: A balanced split
cards.body: If your dataset has 90% healthy patients and 10% sick patients, a Stratified K-Fold split will ensure that every single fold contains exactly 90% healthy and 10% sick patients, preventing unbalanced folds.

cards.title: Does it balance the classes?
cards.body: A common myth is that Stratification balances the overall dataset (making it 50/50). It does not. If your data is 99% class A and 1% class B, each fold will still be 99% A and 1% B. It simply keeps the ratio consistent.

cards.title: Standard vs Stratified Folds
cards.body: Comparing how standard and stratified K-Fold divide imbalanced classes:

left.text:
Purely random.
Some folds get 0 rare cases.
Unstable evaluation.

right.text:
Preserves ratios.
Every fold gets rare cases.
Consistent evaluation.

exercises.prompt: When is Stratified K-Fold most important to use compared to standard K-Fold?

options.text: When dealing with classification tasks with imbalanced target classes
When the dataset has millions of rows and training is slow
When training regression models predicting continuous house prices
When you want to reduce the number of features in your dataset

exercises.explain: Stratification is crucial for classification tasks with imbalanced classes, ensuring every fold represents all classes proportionally.

exercises.prompt: True or false: Stratified K-Fold changes your training data to make the rare class equal in size to the majority class.
exercises.explain: False. It only preserves the existing ratio of classes in each fold; it does not change the ratio or perform resampling.

exercises.sentence: Stratified K-Fold preserves the class ___ in each fold to ensure stable evaluation of rare classes.
exercises.explain: By keeping the ratio consistent, each fold becomes a miniature, representative version of the overall dataset.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following distributions across 3 validation folds shows a correctly stratified split for a dataset with 80% Class A and 20% Class B?
exercises.explain: A stratified split ensures every single fold retains the exact global class distribution (80% A and 20% B).

exercises.prompt: You are building a credit card fraud detector where only 0.5% of transactions are fraudulent. You use a standard 5-Fold cross-validation. On Fold 3, your model gets 100% accuracy, but you realize Fold 3 contained exactly zero fraud cases. How should you fix this?

options.text: Switch to Stratified 5-Fold cross-validation to guarantee fraud cases are in every fold.
Keep the current setup and celebrate the 100% accuracy on Fold 3.
Delete Fold 3 from your evaluation to hide the issue.
Duplicate the fraud cases manually until they make up 50% of the dataset.

exercises.explain: Standard K-Fold can leave rare classes out of some folds entirely. Stratified K-Fold guarantees that fraud cases are distributed proportionally across all folds, making your evaluations honest! 🛡️
